def: v.n. To vomit (i.q. ubaknā, q.v.). v.t. To vomit, reject.
v.n. To fall short, fail (of, a duty, &c.), to miss, slip, blunder ( = ćūknā, q.v.)
more: v. n. 1. To miss; make a slip or mistake; trip. 2. To pass by, over, or away. Āj kā meṅh ūk gayā. ūke chūke, bhūle chūke, bhūle bisre , adv. 1. Unwittingly; accidentally; by mistake. 2. Now and again; now and then; occasionally. Ūke chūke to milā karo .
v. n. To miss, to err, to trip, to mistake.
subs: adv. By mistake, inadvertently, unwittingly; now and then, once in a way.

def: rustic for اسے use or اسکو usko, dat. of وه wǒh.
adj. Sitting on the hams with the soles of the feet on the ground, squatting
more: s. m. The posture of sitting on the hams with the soles of the feet on the ground.

def: v. n. H. u up, goḍoṅ, goṛon knees; S. उत्कटुक + उपविष्ट sitting. To sit on one's hams or heels with the knees raised upward. Kālā ḍālūṅ, lāl nikālūṅ, Ukṛūṅ baiṭh paṭā paṭ mārūṅ . Riddle. Ans. lohā , iron. (Also a double entendre .). Black I put in, red out I bring, Both heels upraised, I make it ring.
adj. Sitting on the hams with the soles of the feet on the ground, squatting
subs: v.n. To sit on one's hams, &c., to squat.

ety: S. उद्+कर्षणं, rt. कृष्; but cf. S. उत्सह्
def: v.t. To raise, lift; to trim (a lamp, by raising the wick); to kindle (a fire), fan (a flame), blow (fire), stir up (embers); to rouse, stir up, move, excite, stimulate, urge; to encourage, incite, instigate.
more: Brij. v. a. S. उत up, कस् to go, move. 1. To raise; hoist; lift; give a lift. 2. To raise (the wick of a candle); kindle; fan the flame; blow (the coals); stir the embers ( bhaṛkānā ). Dȳā uskā de . 3. To give a fillip to; drive; goad; spur; prick. 4. To induce; egg on; work upon; persuade; rouse; encourage; incite; prompt; arouse. Uksā uksā-ke bhagā le gaī . 5. To move; lead; prevail upon; influence; exhort. 6. To instigate; put up; excite; stimulate. Kis ne uksā-ke laṛwā dȳā? 7. To excite; encourage; incense; provoke; inflame.
(caus. of اکسنا ukasnā ) v. a. To excite, stir up.

def: Brij. n. m. 1. One who reminds another of an obligation. 2. A low or shallow person; a mean, scurvy fellow. Nakṭe kā khāīye, ukṭe kā na khāīye . Prov. Eat from the dish of the disgraced; but eat not of his who'll cast it in your teeth. ukaṭwā'nā, ugatwānā , v. a. Caus. of ukaṭnā .
part. adj. Spoken, said, aforesaid, above-mentioned, the said (used in law documents).
s.m. One who casts an obligation in the teeth of; a low, shabby fellow.

v.n. To be sad, sorrowful, melancholy, dejected, &c.; to fret, be irritated (by, -se), chafe (at or under), be out of humour (with, -se); to be languid, be weary (of), be sick (of), to lose all relish (for), to be satiated or cloyed; to become irksome, wearisome, &c.
more: v. a. S. उत कठ् to live in distress. 1. To be weary or tired of; lose one's relish for; to hate. Phr. To be bored, wearied, etc. to death. 1. Roz dāl khāte khāte jī uktā gayā . 2. Māyā kā lāge lob, Nikās nikās kaheṅ log, Sab sote hī uktāeṅge . Kabīr. Greed itches on your wealth to fall, "Away with him, away," they bawl, Your eyes once closed, how weary all. ☞ They are impatient to be rid of the corpse of the man whose wealth they long to possess. 2. To be satiated, cloyed, disgusted; to feel a distaste for. Phr. To grow tired of; feel weary of. 3. To become irksome, tiresome, wearisome, tedious.
v. n. 1. To fret, to be melancholy or dejected. 2. To be out of humour with, to be tired of, to tire.

def: v.t. = اکٿنا ukaṭnā, q.v. v.t. To dig up, turn up, cut up, root up; to unearth; to turn over, turn upside down, upset, turn topsy-turvy; to extract (a secret) gradually and artfully, worm out (a secret); to cast an obligation in one's teeth; to mar, ruin, destroy, spoil, plunder; to rob the good name of, to vilify, abuse; — v. int. To be dug up, turned, cut up, &c.: ukaṭ-ḍālna, v.t. (intens. of ukaṭnā, — generally used in the sense of), to tear, tear to pieces, root up, utterly ruin or destroy, &c.
more: (perhaps, उत् and r. कड Break, separate) v. a. To dig up slowly a thing buried in the ground, to extract a secret gradually and artfully, to pump.

def: v.t. To stir up, rouse, instigate, incite; to support, aid, back, abet; to forward, advance, promote.
more: v. a. To promote, to forward.

def: s.m. Instigator, inciter, aider, abettor; one who forwards or promotes, promoter.
part. Instigator, promoter.

s.f. = اکت ukat or ukti, q.v. s.f. Speaking, speech, voice, language; story, fabrication, invention, contrivance; inventive power, originality, genius (in these last senses the word is probably derived from the S. udgati; see ugat)
subs: To make up a story, to concoct, fabricate, invent, contrive
To do or perform by means of one's inventive power, to invent, originate, create.

def: s.f. Speaking, speech, voice, language; story, fabrication, invention, contrivance; inventive power, originality, genius (in these last senses the word is probably derived from the S. udgati; see ugat)
subs: To make up a story, to concoct, fabricate, invent, contrive
To do or perform by means of one's inventive power, to invent, originate, create.

def: s.f. Punishment, chastisement, torture, torment.

(from عقب) s. f. Punishment, torment, chastisement, torture.

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻaql), Intellects, understandings, minds; sciences.
adv. Conjecturally, at a guess, at a rough estimate or calculation.
adv. H. āṅk measure. By guess; at a rough estimate or calculation.
adj. fem. of awwal, q.v.
more: adj. f. (of اول) First, principal, best.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُولَى (ʔūlā).

def: v. a. (dakh.) See الچنا ulachnā.
v.t. = ulaćnā, q.v. v.t. To throw out, draw off, pour off (water); to bale out; to drain ( = ulīćnā, q.v.).
v.t. To throw out, draw off, pour off (water); to bale out; to drain ( = ulīćnā, q.v.).
more: v. a. To throw out the water from any thing, to drain.

def: s. m. An owl.
v.t. = ulāngnā and ulangnā, q.q.v. v.t. To leap over or across, pass over or beyond, to cross; to bestride, mount, ride (a horse).
s.m. Twitting, casting in one's teeth; reproach; complaint, accusation ( = ulahnā)
more: s. m. Reproach, complaint. See ارهنا urihnā, and الہنا ulahnā.
subs: v.t. To twit, taunt, reproach; to accuse, complain against.

def: s.m. = ulāhnā, q.v. s.m. Twitting, casting in one's teeth; reproach; complaint, accusation ( = ulahnā)
more: 1. s. m. A complaint, accusation: see الاهنا ulāhnā. الہنا دینا ulahnā denā, To reproach, complain (to), accuse. 2. S̱. ( उत् and r. रुह Grow) v. n. To grow, to vegetate.
subs: v.t. To twit, taunt, reproach; to accuse, complain against.

ety: ulah˚ = Prk. उल्लह(इ) = S. उल्लभ(ते), rt. उद्+लभ्
def: v.n. To spring up, grow, vegetate; to reprove; to reproach, &c. (see ulāhnā; urěhnā).

Māṛ, S. उपालम्भ from उप + आ + लाभ् to censure, revile. Casting in one's teeth; a reproach; complaint. ulāh'nā de'nā , v. a. To reproach; accuse; complain against. Bāt bigāṛī āp se, hamīṅ ulāhnā det . Song.
v. n. To be entangled, to ravel, &c. See الجهنا ulajhnā.

ety: by metathesis for ujhalnā = S. उद+झट्: — Other forms are wrajhnā, arajhnā, arujhnā
def: v.n. To be entangled, ravelled, twisted, entwined; to be complicated, made intricate; to be perplexed; to be involved (in difficulties, &c.), to be at a loss; to fall foul (of, -se), dispute or wrangle (with); to interpose, interfere; to demand a reason (of, -se); to join in (with, -se) to cross-question; to be confused, confounded, disordered, deranged, jumbled; to be entrapped, ensnared, inveigled, enticed; to be captured, taken, shut up, confined, imprisoned; to be laid out, be invested (capital); to stick, be unable to proceed (in an affair, lesson, &c.); to have the heart set or fixed (on, -se), be enamoured (of), be captivated or charmed (with); to have a liaison (with), form an illicit connection (with); to be engaged (to), betrothed (to); to be married; to be busy, occupied, engaged (in, -meṅ), be intent (on); to carp or cavil (at, -par), find fault (with)
more: v. n. Opp. of sulajh'nā . 1. To be entangled, ravelled, knotted, twisted, tangled. Sāre bāl ulajh gaë . 2. To be complicated, perplexed, made intricate. 3. To dispute ( jhagaṛnā ); fall out; wrangle; quarrel. Ham se mat uljho, nahīṅ to bāṅḍī chal jāëgī . 4. To be deranged, disordered, tossed, thrown into disorder. Ulajhnā āsān, sulajhnā mushkil . Prov. Easy to entangle, hard to disentangle. 5. To be involved in difficulties; to be at a loss. 6. To be ensnared, entrapped, caught in a net. Machchhī jā! meṅ ulaih gaī . 7. To be captured, taken, imprisoned ( phaṅsnā ). Ham muqadme meṅ nāhaq ulajh gaë . 8. To be betrothed, tied in wedlock, married. Jab ulajh jāëgā, to āp hī sulajh rahegā . Caught in her net, sober he'll get. 9. To fall in love; to be entangled in a love affair. 1. Rām kare, kahīṅ nainā na urjhe . Song. O Rām, let not these eyes be entangled in the meshes of desire. 2. Merā jy…
1. v. n. To be entangled, to ravel, to be involved (in difficulties). 2. v. a. To quarrel, debate, cavil.
subs: (-se), To join in, close (with), fall foul (of), dispute (with), cavil (at); to interpose, interfere, &c. (see ulajhnā)
v.n. To be delayed or detained; to delay, tarry, linger, stick.

def: n. m. A kind of boat.
s.m. A kind of small boat.
more: s. f. A kind of small boat.

def: adj. = ulāṛ, q.v. adj. Liable to overturn, unstable; too heavily laden at the back (a cart), not having the burden equally disposed (a cart, &c.), top-heavy ( = ulāl).
v.t. = ulāṛnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to lie down, to turn over on the side, to cause to sleep.
more: (caus. of اللنا ulalnā, q. v.) v. a. To overset, to lay over to one side.

def: v.n. = ulaṛnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to lie down, to turn over on the side, to cause to sleep.
more: v. n. To be overset. See ulaṛ'nā .
v. n. To be overset, to be laid on one side.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻālim), The learned; doctors of law and religion.
more: s. plur. (of عالم āﻌlim ) The learned.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُلَمَاء (ulamā'), borrowed from Arabic عُلَمَاء (ʕulamāʔ), from عَالِم (ʕālim), from عَلِمَ (ʕalima).

def: s.m. Hard swelling; tumour; swollen gland.
more: E. n. m. A hard swelling; a swollen gland; a knot.

def: v.t. = uleṇḍnā, q.v. v.t. To invert a vessel in order to empty it of its contents; to empty; to pour out (water, &c. = unḍelnā, q.v.).
adj. Naked, nude.
v.t. To leap over or across, pass over or beyond, to cross; to bestride, mount, ride (a horse).
= ulangnā, q.v. v.t. To leap over or across, pass over or beyond, to cross; to bestride, mount, ride (a horse).
v.n. To leap, pass or go (over); — v.t. To leap over or across; to go beyond, overstep (see ulāngnā).
v.t. To leap over or across, pass over or beyond, to cross; to bestride, mount, ride (a horse).
more: Bhoj.; v. a. S. लङ्घ--- to leap. 1. To leap over; cross; clear; run across. Billī ulāṅg-ke to nahīṅ āë, jo jhāṅj lāè .! Wom. You have n't crossed a cat's path that you are so quarrelsome! 2. To pass over; overlook; disregard. Huqqā ulāṅgtā nahīṅ hai . Pass the hookah fair. i. e. dont pass the next man over. 3. To ride; mount; cross (a horse). Ghoṛā ulāṅgnā . 4. Used as a double entendre. Naī bachherī ko ulāṅgo, jo kuchh dam hai .

def: adj. Fat, plump, in good condition.
more: E. adj. Plump; fat; in good case.

def: adj. Liable to overturn, unstable; too heavily laden at the back (a cart), not having the burden equally disposed (a cart, &c.), top-heavy ( = ulāl).
adj. S. उत् up, लुल् H. luṛhnā to roll. Liable to overturn; overladen at the back (a cart). Gāṛī ulāṛ hai, āge ko baiṭho .

ety: S. परा+वर्तनीयं, rt. वृत्; but cf. S. उल्लाल, rt. लल्
def: v.t. To overturn, upset, tilt-over, lay over to one side. (cf. ulaṭnā.)
v.t. To cause to lie down, to turn over on the side, to cause to sleep.
more: v. a. To overset; upset; tilt over. g—r ulāṛ-ke paṛ rah'nā , v. n. To lie down on one's belly; to lie down; loll; sleep; to be in a sound sleep.
(trans. of الرنا) v. a. To cause to sleep.

def: v.n. To lie down, to rest.
more: v. n. S. उत up, लुल् to roll about. 1. To fall over, or backwards; tilt over; capsize. 2. To lay down; fall down; to be floored, thrown on one's back.
v. n. To lie down, to rest.

def: v.n. To topple over, fall over, roll over, tilt over, upset; to fall backwards, fall down, be thrown on the back; to lie over on one side; to careen (as a ship).
s.m. Leavings of food from the table of a great personage (of which the servants partake)
more: n. The leavings of food that has been partaken of by a great personage, or by a spiritual guide. ulash kar'nā , v. a. To touch; taste (food); to take a mouthful.
s. m. The leavings of a superiour.
subs: v.t. To touch or taste (food); to take a morsel.

ety: S. परि or परा+वर्त rt. वृत्; cf. H. palaṭ
def: s.m. (f.?), Overturning, upsetting; reverting; inversion, reversal; inverse, reverse, opposite; contrary; the wrong side; return; subversion; change
subs: s.m. Confusion, jumble; rout and riot; interchange; deception, imposition, cheating; — adj. Higgledy-piggledy, topsy-turvy, &c. ( = ulṭā-pulṭā, q.v.)
v.t. To throw into confusion, to toss about, turn topsy-turvy, &c.
To be in a state of confusion, &c.
ulaṭ-peć (or peṅć), s.m. Perplexity, embarrassment; intricacy, maze, entanglement, difficulty; adversity, misfortune; turn or vicissitude of fortune; perversion; deception, trickery
and ulaṭ-peć-kā, adj. (f. -kī), Perplexing, intricate, labyrinthine; entangling, crooked, crafty (as conduct or speech)
s.m. Name of a certain plant and its flower, Abroma augustum.

def: n. m. 1. Inversion; reversion; reversal. 2. The opposite; contrary; reverse; inverse. 3. The wrong side. 4. Subversion; vicissitude; change. ulaṭ bed, labed' , n. m. What is contrary to the Ved. ulaṭ pech, ulaṭ peṅch, ulaṭ pher , n. m. 1. Embarrassment; perplexity; dilemma. 2. Intricacy; labyrinth; net. 3. Mishap; misfortune; misery. 4. The wheel of fortune; vicissitudes of fortune. 5. Mess; scrape; difficulty. ulaṭ pech kī bāteṅ, ulaṭ pher kī bāteṅ , n. f. Crooked or crafty speech; the labyrinth of false philosophy. Bandī ye tumhārī ulaṭ pech kī bāteṅ nahīṅ samajhtī . Mah. Wom. ulat pech meṅ phaṅsnā , v. n. To be entangled in difficulties; to be in a pickle.
(dakh.) i. q. مغرورهونا To be or to become proud (?).
1. adj. Higgledy-piggledy, topsyturvy. 2. s. f. Confusion, interchange.
more: s. (dakh.) Confusion; topsyturvy, upside down, higgledy-piggledy (?).
wety: From الٹنا (ulaṭnā) + پلٹنا (palaṭnā).

def: v.n. To become proud, to have one's head turned.
v. (dakh.) See الٿنا ulaṭnā.

v.t. & n. = ulaṭnā, q.v. v.t. To overturn, upset, capsize, turn upside down, turn inside out, to invert, reverse; to subvert, overthrow, throw down, ruin, destroy; to throw (as a horse its rider); to turn over, prostrate, bring low, overcome, reduce to a state of helplessness or poverty; to throw up, vomit; to turn up, to dig; to change, pervert (meaning or sense); to recall, retract; to refute, rebut, contradict (anything said, e.g. bāt ulaṭnā); to pour (into); to pour out, empty; to toss or drink off; to send back, to return; to turn over (leaves of a book), go through (a book); to read over again; to revolve (in the mind); to transpose; to translate; to counteract; to act contrary to (orders, &c.), to disobey; to reverse (a decision); to cancel, rescind; — v.n. To be overturned, to be turned upside down, &c.; to topple over, to upset; to roll over, fall down; to change; to be changed, perverted, misrepresented, misconstrued; to be reversed or transferred (as a charm); to be dug up; to go or come back, to return; to turn (against, -se), revolt (from), rebel (against); to have (one's) head turned, to become proud; to be perturbed or agitated (the heart); to go mad; to be reduced to wretchedness or poverty, to be ruined, become bankrupt
more: v. a. or n. (dakh.) To turn, &c.: see الٿنا ulaṭnā.
subs: v.n. To fall down, fall over, &c. ( = ulaṭnā); to turn (upon or against, -par), to be opposed (to); to flock (to, -meṅ), go in crowds (to)
v.n. = ulaṭnā, and is the more common form
v.t. intensive of ulaṭnā, and is the more common form
adv. Over again, e.g. ulaṭ-ke kahnā, To say over again, to repeat.

def: v.n. To undulate; to be agitated (as the ocean by storms).
more: v. n. To undulate, to be agitated (as the ocean by storms).

ety: S. परा+वर्तनीयं, rt. वृत्, — cf. palaṭnā and lauṭnā
def: v.t. To overturn, upset, capsize, turn upside down, turn inside out, to invert, reverse; to subvert, overthrow, throw down, ruin, destroy; to throw (as a horse its rider); to turn over, prostrate, bring low, overcome, reduce to a state of helplessness or poverty; to throw up, vomit; to turn up, to dig; to change, pervert (meaning or sense); to recall, retract; to refute, rebut, contradict (anything said, e.g. bāt ulaṭnā); to pour (into); to pour out, empty; to toss or drink off; to send back, to return; to turn over (leaves of a book), go through (a book); to read over again; to revolve (in the mind); to transpose; to translate; to counteract; to act contrary to (orders, &c.), to disobey; to reverse (a decision); to cancel, rescind; — v.n. To be overturned, to be turned upside down, &c.; to topple over, to upset; to roll over, fall down; to change; to be changed, perverted, misrepresented, misconstrued; to be reversed or transferred (as a charm); to be dug up; to go or come back, to return; to turn (against, -se), revolt (from), rebel (against); to have (one's) head turned, to become proud; to be perturbed or agitated (the heart); to go mad; to be reduced to wretchedness or poverty, to be ruined, become bankrupt
more: v. a. To pervert, subvert, thwart, reverse, overturn, reply. v. n. To be reversed, turned over or turned back, to return.
subs: v.n. To fall down, fall over, &c. ( = ulaṭnā); to turn (upon or against, -par), to be opposed (to); to flock (to, -meṅ), go in crowds (to)
v.n. = ulaṭnā, and is the more common form
v.t. intensive of ulaṭnā, and is the more common form

wety: Inherited from Old Hindi उलटना (ulaṭanā), a verbal derivative from Prakrit उल्लट्ट (ullaṭṭa, “turned over”). Related to پلٹنا.

def: v. (dakh.) See الٿنا ulaṭnā.

r: ulaṭ'nā, ulaṭ denā, ulṭā denā, ulaṭ ḍāl'nā
def: v. a. 1. To turn over; reverse; invert. 2. To turn upside down; to subvert; overthrow; overturn; tilt over; capsize. 3. To throw on one's back. Ghoṛe ne ulaṭ dȳā . 4. To vomit; spew; cast up ( ulṭī k .). Davā pīte hī ulaṭ dī . 5. To dig and turn up (the soil); to plough ( jotnā ). Pahar bhar dabā-ke hal jotā, sārī dhartī ulaṭ dī . 6. To pervert the meaning ( arth ulaṭnā ). 7. To pour in; fill ( uṅḍelnā ). Rahī sahī is meṅ ulaṭ de . 8. To drink ( pīnā ); to toss off. Samandar-sokh ne sārī botal ulaṭ ḍālī . 9. To pour out ( uṅḍelnā ); to empty. 10. To return; send back ( wāpis k .). 11. To make one drunk or intoxicated ( matwālā k .) Brāṅḍī ne ulaṭ dȳā . 12. To cause to fall into a swoon; to produce madness. Saīyad bāchchhā ne ulaṭ dȳo . Rus. 13. To enfeeble; weaken; enervate. Chār dinā ke tāo ne ulaṭ dȳo . Rus. 14. To destroy; pull down; demolish. Khitte kā khittā ulaṭ dȳā . 15. To ruin; to bring or reduce to poverty. Dhī beṭī kā kharach ādmī ko ulaṭ detā hai . 16. To repeat; recite; rehearse. Yeh kahānī ulaṭ-ke kaho . 17. To read over; to go through; finish. Chār din meṅ sārī kitāb ulat ḍālī . 18. To revise; read again ( dohrānā .) 19. To turn over (the leaves); pretend to read. Paṛhte nahiṅ to varq ulaṭne se kyā fāëdā? 20. To think over; reflect ( bichārnā ); revolve. 21. To change; transpose; transfer ( badalnā ). 22. To translate ( tarjumah k .); render; turn into. 23. To treat as false; impute falsehood to; contradict. 24. Not to agree or accede to; refuse; to say no. 25. To counter-charm; counter-spell. Mantar ulaṭ dȳā . 26. To go against; disobey; violate; infriuge. Hamārā kahnā ulaṭ dȳā . 27. To cancel; overrule; rescind; set aside. Unkā hukm ulaṭ dȳā. bāt ulaṭ'nā, bāt pher'nā , v. a. 1. To unsay; deny; retract; prevaricate. 2. To refute; confute; disprove; rebut.
more: v. n. 1. To be reversed, inverted, turned over or upside down, capsized. Kāṭte hī ulaṭ gayā . 2. To topple over; tilt over. Gāṛī ulaṭgaī . 2. To be reversed or transferred (the effect of a charm). Mantar ulaṭ gayā . 4. To be perverted, misconstrued, misrepresented. 5. To dig up; to be ploughed, tilled. Sārī dhartī ulṭī parī hai, pānī paṛe to bīj ḍāleṅ . 6. To go or come back; return ( lauṭ ānā ). Ulaṭ tum ghaṛ ko jāo, bhāī . 7. To turn upon or against; to be opposed to. Āj hākim usī pe ulaṭ paṛā . 8. To turn against; revolt; rebel against; mutiny. Ek jahān ham se ulaṭ gayā . 9. To fall upon; go at ( Slang ). Khāk chāṭ-ke ulaṭ gayā . 10. To change; to be changed ( badalnā ). 11. To be intoxicated or drunk ( matwālā honā ). Pīte hī ulaṭ gayā . 12. To turn mad, insane, crazy. Dil ulaṭ gayā . 13. To become unfortunate; to fall on evil days; to come under an evil star. Jā-ke gulzār se saīyād …

def: v.t. To invert a vessel in order to empty it of its contents; to empty; to pour out (water, &c. = unḍelnā, q.v.).

def: v.t. To cause to leap over, cause to go beyond.

s.f. Leaping over. (ruśtic.)

def: adj. (f. -ī), Entangled, complicated, involved, intricate

s.f. = H الجهاو उलझाव uljhāʼo, s.m. = H الجهاوا उलझावा uljhāwā, s.m. Entanglement. complication, intricacy, involution; perplexity, anxiety, uneasiness; twist, ply, turn, windings and turnings, maze; doubling (as of a hare); confusion, disorder, derangement, disturbance; embroilment, imbroglio; difficulty, embarrassment; discord, misunderstanding, jar.

r: uljhānā, iljhānā, ulajhnā, urjhānā, irjhānā, arujhānā, arjhānā, ajhurānā
ety: caus. of ulajhnā, q.v. — Other forms are urjhānā, irjhānā, arujhānā, arjhānā, ajhurānā
def: v.t. To entangle, ravel (as thread); to involve, complicate, make intricate; to perplex, confound; to implicate; to entrap, insnare; to inveigle, entice, allure, beguile, mislead, deceive; to divert, entertain; to double (as a hare); to embroil, to involve in a quarrel; to make (one's wits) whirl round; to set (one's brain) to work; to throw into confusion, disarrange, jumble; to intertwine, interlace, to fasten (to, -se), connect (with), join (to); to unite by marriage; to put (one into, -meṅ, a berth); to shut up, commit to prison; to lay out, invest (capital); to put on (a garment, hastily or temporarily)
more: Opp. of suljhā'nā' ; Tir. ujhrā'yab ; Mag. and Bhoj. arujhā nā, a jhurā'nā' ; Brij. urjhā'nā, irjhānā , v. a. 1. To tangle; entangle; ravel; knot; mat. 2. To disarrange; confuse; jumble; muddle; tumble; bring, put, or throw into disorder. Gorak-dhandā uljhā aȳā . 3. To involve; complicate; make intricate; perplex. Hote huāte kām ko ūljhā dȳā . 4. To cause to whirl round (one's wits or brain). 5. To embroil; set against; make a quarrel ( laṛānā ). Phr. To set together by the ears. Aisā donoṅ ko iljh/?/ūṅ, jo khūb jūt patraṅg ho . 6. To fasten; bind; tether; chain; link; couple; brace; bracket; interlace; intertwine. Kaṛī se kaṛī uljhā do . 7. To stitch; thread; sew; pin. 8. To catch in a trap or net; ensnare. Kabūtar phāṅd meṅ iljhā lo . 9. To imprison; send or commit to prison. Hākim ne do mahīne ko iljhā dȳā . 10. To betroth; give in marriage; bind in wedlock. Laṛke ko kahīṅ uljhā do…
(trans. of الجهنا) v. a. To entangle, to ravel (as, thread), to involve, busy or engage (in difficulties, business, &c.), to embroil.
subs: v.t. To keep (one) diverted or beguiled, &c., to detain, delay
(and urjhānā-surjhānā), v.n. To be tossed about; to flutter, flap (as a flag).

def: s. m. Entanglement, perplexity, intricacy, complication, involution.

more: n. 1. Twist; tangled web; labyrinth; windings and turnings ( pher phār ); maze. 2. Entanglement; complication; complexity; intricacy; imbroglio. Ul heṛe kī bāt na karo . 3. Perplexity; care; trouble; uneasiness. Rahī uljhan sī tā sahar usko, N/?/ṅ l āī na rāt bhar usko . Shauq. 4. Derangement; disorder; disarrangement; disturbance. Phr. Wheels within wheels; confusion worse confounded. 5. Misunderstanding; discord; jar; animosity. Diloṅ kā uljhāo Khudā khole . 6. Row; riot; rumpus; embroilment. 7. Difficulty; embarrassment; scrape; mess. Baṛe uljheṛe meṅ phaṅse haiṅ, Khudā hī nikāle. aljheṛ' yā , n. m. A disputatious or quarrelsome person a wrangler.

def: s.m. Jumping up, skipping; alacrity; gladness, joy, happiness; splendour, light; division of a book, chapter, section.
s.m. An owl; (met.) a stupid fellow, blockhead, fool; (in gamblers' parlance) one to be rooked, a 'pigeon'; — adj. Stupid, foolish, senseless
more: n. m. S. उलूकः. 1. An owl; the great-eared or horned owl; the bird of night. 2. A stupid fellow; blockhead; fool; simpleton. Ullū kī dum fākhtah! Prov. An owl to a dove! 3. A sign of desolation and ruin. Ullū bol gayā. lit . The owl has hooted. Ruin reigns around. 4. Winnings; harvest; gains. Apnā ullī kahīṅ nahīṅ gayā . Prov. My pigeon has not flown away (I have still some one to rook). ul'lū , adj. Stupid; foolish; idiotic. Ullū māṅ ke khabtī beṭe . A stupid mother, a witless son. ullū banānā , v. a. See ahmaq banānā (1. 2). To befool; cheat. Jorū ne uskī usko hai ullū khilā dȳā, Hai us harām-khor ko ullū banā dȳā . Zafar. His wife did make him owl-flesh eat, An oaf she made of him complete. ☞ The natives, and Mah. Women especially, believe that a man who eats the flesh of an owl becomes the obedient slave of his wife. ullū bannā, ullū honā , v. n. To be intoxicated. Chullū meṅ ullū,…
n. m. A tree so called, the natives say, because it is a favourite perch of the owl. Saccharum cylindricum . Wat.
s. m. An owl; (metaph.) a stupid fellow, a blockhead, a fool.
subs: v.t. To make a fool of, to befool; to take in, gull, cheat
ullū honā, v.n. To become a fool, make a fool of oneself, stultify oneself; to be intoxicated
v.t. To entrap a green fellow, catch a gull, rook a pigeon
s.m. lit. 'A young owl'; an arrant fool, a great idiot

lit. 'To eat the flesh of an owl'; to become foolish, stupid, or idiotic.

def: (caus. of الٿهنا) v. a. (dakh.) To turn, &c.: see الٿانا ulṭānā.
(dakh.) Returning, on return.

adj. See ulṭā. ulṭe pāoṅ phir'nā, ulte pāoṅ phir ānā , v. n. To retrace one's steps; to go, come, turn, or get back immediately; counter-march. Phr. To turn upon one's heels; to beat a retreat; to dance the back step. Is kām ko kar-ke ulṭe pāoṅ phir ānā. ulṭī paṭṭī paṛhā'nā , v. a. 1. To misteach; mislead; pervert one's mind; poison one's ear. Nasīb sīdhā agar hai merā, lachaktī niklegī khāṭ uskī! Woh sukh na pāegī jis ne bhejā hai, ulṭī paṭṭī tumheṅ paṛhā-kar! Mah. Wom. Jān Sāhab. Be good my fate—these eyes shall see her trembling bier! No rest for her who warped your mind and sent you here! (May she be struck dead, plump and well conditioned, her bier trembling under the weight of her corpse). 2. To set against; estrange; alienate; turn away one's affections. ulṭī chhāonī bāndhnā, lit . To lay on the thatch the wrong way. To criminate unjustly. ulṭī chīn . A kind of coating for the huqqā tube. ulṭī rāt kā , n. m. A fool; simpleton. ulṭī rīt, ku-rīt , n. f. 1. Unlawful, improper, or unconstitutional mode or way. 2. Peculiar or uncertain temper; caprice. Rājā, jogī, agan. jal. inkī ulṭī rīt, Ḍartā rahīo Paras Rām, ye thoṛī pāleṅ prīt. Dohā . Princes and priests, water and fire, capricions are, Fear them, Paras Rām, but little love they bear. ulṭe sāṅs lenā , v. n. To gasp; breathe convulsively. Ulṭe sāṅs lene lagā, ab kyā bachegā? ulṭī sam'ajh, ulṭī mat , n. f. 1. A wrong view or opinion; error; fallacy. 2. Misconception; misapprehension; misunderstanding. Aurat kī mat ulṭī . Prov. Women are always wrong. 3. Defective or weak understanding. ulṭī sīdhī sunānā, auṅdhī sīdhī sunānā , v. a. To revile; vilify; abuse ( gālī denā ); insult. Us se bole, to woh ulṭī sīdhī sunāëgā . ulṭī sai'fī , Mah. n. f. A curse or malediction which falls on the utterer. ☞ The person who pronounces the curse, places a naked sword erect before him, blowing on the sword after every word and sentence. The effect is believed to be the death or ruin of the person so cursed. But if the curse recoils on the person who pronounced it, it is called ulṭī saifi (the biter bit). Saifi is a form of imprecation against an enemy. ulṭī taraf . The back; the other side; the reverse. ulṭe kānṭe. lit . The power arm slightly bent. Somewhat short weight. ulṭī kar'nā , v. a. To vomit; cast up. ulṭī khoprī, auṅdhā gyān , Prov. Crooked head, crooked mind. ulṭī lāg , A. nisbat-i-aks ( Math ). Invertendo. ulṭī mār , A reverse blow; a back stroke. ulṭī mālā pher nā , v. n. lit . To count one's beads backwards. To call down curses upon one. Aisī ulṭī mālā pherī, nās ho gayā. ulṭe mulk kā . Belonging to an ignorant, undiscerning race or nation. Ajab ulṭe mulk ke haiṅ, ajī āp bhī, ḳi tum se Kabhī bāt kī jo sīdhī, to milā javāb ulṭā . Inshā. Yours surely is a strangely crooked race, Speak you but fair, you cut up rough apace. ulṭī havā , n. f. Contrary winds.
s.f. A kind of grass = ulū and ulūʼa, q.q.v. pron. adj. contr. from اولو ulū, q.v. (The word occurs in compounds, as uluʼl-amr, &c.)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُولُو (ʔulū).

def: pron. adj. contr. from اولو ulū, q.v. (The word occurs in compounds, as uluʼl-amr, &c.)

def: v. n. S. उ up, मङ्ग् to move swiftly, 1. To swell; flow or run over; overflow ( umaṅgnā ). 1. Jhaṛ kar rahīn haiṅ chiṛyāṅ, nāle umaṅḍ rahe haiṅ, Barse hai meṅh jhaṛā jhaṛ, bādal ghumaṅḍ rahe haiṅ . Nazīr. The birds chirp merrily, the streamlets swell and bound along, The rain comes down in show'rs, the sky with gath'ring clouds is hung. 2. Sāvan āvan kah gaë, umag chale baho nīr, Jo ab-ke pī daras deṅ to sītal hoëṅ sarīr. Bārā-māsā . Khairā Shāh. In July he'd return, he said, rain showers in torrents pour, Calm to this breast should come again could I see him once more. 2. To be overhung; to overwhelm; overspread; overpower. Barse thā meṅh bhī jhūm jhūm, chhājoṅ umaṅḍ umaṅḍ paṛā, Jhoke havā ke chal rahe, yār bagal meṅ loṭtā . Nazīr. The driving rain wind-tossed in streams o'er-pow'ring swept along, The wind blew gusts; my love rolled in my arms in dalliance long. 3. To flock; crowd; throng; cluster. Bājā baje dhamak se re, bhāī, umṛe gāoṅ ke log . Brij. 4. To be affected, moved, touched, melted; to be full (the heart). Dil umaṅḍ āyā hamārā abr-i-naisāṅ dekh-kar. umaṅḍ umaṅḍ-kar ronā v. n. To weep violently or bitterly; to cry aloud. Phr. To cry one's eyes out.
s.f. Excessive joy, elation, rapture, transport; exultation, intoxication (of success); pride, conceit; excessive desire, longing; ambition. (Also written umag.)
more: n. f. S. उ up मङ्ग to go, move; मराठ् to desire. 1. Excessive joy; elation; exultation; transport; rapture; ecstasy; bliss. 2. Desire; passion; longing. Phāgan, lagī umaṅg, ishq kā raṅg kheltī saṅg pȳā ke pyārī . Song. Bārā māsā. The spring has come, desire-suffused, to sportive dalliance mated-love given up.
s. f. Excessive joy, transport, exultation, ambition.

def: v.n. To be elated or exultant; to swell with joy or pleasure; to advance with joy or exultingly; — to swell, heave, dilate, expand, fill out; to be flushed or intoxicated (with success).
more: v. n. See umaṅḍ'nā (1). 1. To become large; fill out; develop; distend; heave; flush; glow. 1. Itnā saneswā kahīṅ samjhāë, Umkā joban, mo se rahahu na jāë . Wom. Song. This message only bear to him from me, "My full breasts burst, I cannot live from thee". 2. Morī bārī umaryā, umgo hai joban, Ek rasyā bin hoë gaī dukhyā . Wom. Song. Yet young in years, my bosom swells apace, To suffering doomed—no lover to embrace. 3. Gāt ubhrā, aur umgī kāyā, Hātan meṅ khub gaiṅ chuṛyāṅ ḍhī'ī . Wom. Song. Developed every glowing youthful charm, Bracelets, erst loose, now clasp a rounded arm. 2. To heave or swell with joy; to be delighted, overjoyed.
v. n. To advance with joy.

def: s.f. Life; life-time, period of life; age
more: n. f. Age (H. ārbal ). 1. Ham to apnī umr se yehī dekhte haiṅ . 2. Subah hotī hai, shām hotī hai, umr yūṅ hī tamām hotī hai . The morn no sooner come to evening grows, So life's course all too soon attains its close. umr bhar , adv. For life; during the term of natural life. umr bhar kā jalāpā . A lifelong grievance, etc. umr bhar kī roṭiyāṅ sīdhī k., yā kamānā , v. a. To earn enough for one's whole life; to make a fortune. umr-paṭṭā , A lease for life. Lāë the ham to umr-paṭā yahāṅ likhā, vale Jab siyāhī par safaidī chaṛhī tab khabar paṛī . Old age. Nazīr. Methought a lease of life I'd taken out fair, But when black turned to white I did despair. umr ṭer k., kāṭnā, yā guzārnā, umr ke din bharnā , v. n. To pass one's life; drag on one's days. Martā hūṅ, taṛaptā hūṅ, paṛā hijr meṅ us bin, Din umr ke bhartā hūṅ shab o roz maiṅ gin gin . Nazīr. I writhe and flutter and die a thousand deaths…
s. f. Age, lifetime, period of life. ¶ uﻌmar, n. prop. The name of the second sovereign in succession from Muḥammad.
subs: adv. During the term of natural life; for life
adj. (f. -kī), Life-long; sufficient for a lifetime

wety: Borrowed from Arabic عُمْرَة (ʕumra).

def: corr. of A. umarā, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

ety: inf. n. of عمّ 'to be common,' &c.

def: adj. Less, deficient, wanting, minus; one less than (in which sense it is prefixed to the even decimal numerals from twenty up to a hundred, e.g. un-nīs, one less than twenty, or nineteen; un-tīs, one less than thirty, or twenty-nine, and so on)

def: intj. of interrogation; of anger; of reproach; of contempt.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: s.f. Wool; shaggy hair that may be woven; — gādar ānī ūn-koṅ baiṭhe ćare kapās, 'I bought the sheep for (its) wool, and it does nothing but eat the cotton' (a proverb used where that which is done by way of economy or profit turns out expensive or ruinous).
more: n. The field into which rice has been transplanted.
n. f. S. ऊर्ण Pr. उणं Panj. unn Wool; coarse hair that may be woven. Gādar ānī ūn ko, baiṭhī chare kapās . Prov. The sheep, brought home her wool to yield, Ate up entire the cotton field.

s. f. Wool. گاڐرآني اون کون بيٿهي چري کپاس gāḍar ānī ūn koṅ baiṭhī chare kapās, I brought the sheep for wool and she is eating up the cotton. (Spoken of any thing done by way of economy, which turns out very expensive, or by which a loss is incurred.)

more: adj. S. उच्च high. Opp. of nīchā . 1. Tall; lofty; elevated; exalted; raised; high. Nakṭā būchā, sab se ūṅchā! Prov. No nose, no ears, and yet the highest of the high! 2. Towering; beetling; soaring. 3. Upland; upper; overhanging; steep. 4. Loud; high-sounding; swelling. Phr. At the top of one's voice. Nām baṛā ūṅchā, kān donoṅ būchā! E. Prov. A great name, and both ears cut! (A stain in the family). 5. Great; noble; honorable. Inkā ūṅchā kul hai . 6. Eminent; distinguished; famous. 7. Conspicuous; above-board; open. 8. Ironic . Thick-headed; stupid; foolish. Āo, ūṅche Lālā, baiṭho . Come, long shanks (lack-wit), take a seat. ūṅchā hāth rahnā , v. n. To have the upper hand; to be superior or over; to exceed; excel. Dene se iske terā ūṅchā rahegā hāth . Charity. Nazīr. As thou givest so shall thy hand be uplifted. (You will be enriched so as to give evermore —the depressed hand signifying…

n. f. 1. Unevenness; roughness; ruggedness. Ūṅch nīch meṅ boī kiyārī, jo upjī so bhaī hamārī . Prov. On high and low ground I have sown, all's mine whate'er is grown. 2. Goodness and evil; profit and loss. 1. Baṛā huā to kyā huā? baṛh gayā jaise bāṅs, Ūṅch nīch samjhe nahīṅ, khoyā kul kā nās . Dohā. Grown up! What then? E'en so shoots up a bamboo tall, No sense of good and ill, the house a ruin all. 2. Tum ūṅch nīch nahīṅ samajhte, yūṅ hī kar baiṭhte ho . 3. Ups and downs; vicissitudes; the revolutions or changes of life; the wheel of fortune; ebb and flow; flux and reflux; the whirligig (of time) ( utār chaṛhāo ). Paṛe apne apne jo sab aish bīch, Na samjhe zamāne kī kuchh ūṅch nīch . Mīr Hasan. In pleasure steeped and sunken all, No sense of time's changes at all. ūṅch nīch soch'nā, yā dekh'nā , v. n. 1.
